title: Multi-function transducer assembly and system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a multi-function transducer assembly and system comprising a multi-function transducer assembly, a method of manufacturing the assembly and a method of controlling the system. The assembly includes a piezoelectric transducer layer and spaced first terminals electrically attached to the piezoelectric transducer layer. The spaced first terminals are arranged for supplying an audio frequency signal to the piezoelectric transducer layer so that the assembly can function as a speaker. Second terminals are arranged for receiving at least one signal from the piezoelectric transducer layer or supplying at least one signal to the piezoelectric transducer layer. The second terminals are electrically attached to a region of the piezoelectric transducer layer between the spaced first terminals.
claims:
Claims A multi-function transducer assembly comprising: a piezoelectric transducer layer; at least two spaced first terminals electrically attached to the piezoelectric transducer layer and arranged for supplying an audio frequency signal to the piezoelectric transducer layer; and second terminals arranged for receiving at least one signal from the piezoelectric transducer layer or supplying at least one signal to the piezoelectric transducer layer wherein the second terminals are electrically attached to a region of the piezoelectric transducer layer between the two spaced first terminals. The multi-function transducer assembly of claim 1 , comprising electrically conductive runners electrically coupled to a respective one of the second terminals wherein the electrically conductive runners are electrically insulated from the piezoelectric transducer layer. The multi-function transducer assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the two spaced first terminals are electrically attached to respective peripheral regions of the piezoelectric transducer layer. The multi-function transducer assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the second terminals include a group of at least three said second terminals arranged to define a pressure sensitive touch pad region of the piezoelectric transducer layer. The multi-function transducer assembly of claim 4, wherein the group comprises more than three of said second terminals and wherein the group forms an array on the piezoelectric transducer layer. The multi-function transducer assembly of any preceding claim, wherein two of the said second terminals are switch terminals arranged to define a switch region of the piezoelectric transducer layer wherein the switch region is arranged to generate a switch signal across the switch terminals in response to pressure applied to the switch region. The multi-function transducer assembly of any preceding claim, wherein two of the said second terminals are slider terminals arranged to define a slider function region of the piezoelectric transducer layer wherein the slider function region is arranged to generate a variable voltage signal across the slider terminals in response to pressure applied at different locations on the slider function region. The multi-function transducer assembly of any preceding claim, wherein two of said second terminals are microphone terminals arranged to define a microphone region of the piezoelectric transducer layer wherein the microphone region is arranged to generate audio frequency signals across microphone terminals in response to sound waves deforming the microphone region. The multi-function transducer assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the piezoelectric transducer layer comprises ceramic piezoelectric crystals. The multi-function transducer assembly of claims 2 to 9, wherein the electrically conductive runners are formed in a foldable insulating membrane The multi-function transducer assembly of claim 10, wherein the foldable insulating membrane is an insulating substrate of a circuit board. A multi-function transducer system comprising the multi-function transducer assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the system includes speaker driving circuitry coupled to the spaced first terminals. The system of claim 12, including signal processing circuitry coupled to at least two of the second terminals. The system of claim 12 or claim 13, including haptic feedback driving circuitry coupled to at least two of the second terminals. The system of claims 12 to 14 arranged to control the speaker driving circuitry to selectively supply an audio signal to the spaced first terminals to thereby vibrate the piezoelectric transducer layer and emit audio waves. 16. The system of claims 12 to 15 including signal processing circuitry arranged to process voltages received from at least two of the second terminals in response to a distortion of the piezoelectric transducer layer by a mechanical force applied thereto. 17. The system of claim 14, wherein the haptic feedback driving circuitry is arranged to supply a signal to at least some of the second terminals to thereby distort the piezoelectric transducer layer to provide a haptic feedback function. 18. A control panel comprising the multi-function transducer assembly of claims 1 to 1 1 . 19. A vehicle comprising the multi-function transducer assembly of claims 1 to 1 1 . 20. A method of manufacturing a multi-function transducer assembly, the method comprising: providing a substrate comprising at least two spaced first terminals and at least two second terminals; and electrically attaching to a piezoelectric transducer layer the at least two spaced first terminals and the at least two second terminals, wherein the second terminals are electrically attached to a region of the piezoelectric transducer layer between the two spaced first terminals. 21 . The method of manufacturing a multi-function transducer assembly of claim 20, wherein the electrically attaching is characterised in that the spaced first terminals are electrically attached to respective peripheral regions of the piezoelectric transducer layer. 22. The method of manufacturing a multi-function transducer assembly of claim 20 or 21 , wherein the substrate includes electrically conductive runners electrically coupled to a respective one of the second terminals, wherein the electrically conductive runners are electrically insulated from the piezoelectric transducer layer. 23. The method of manufacturing a multi-function transducer assembly of claims 20 to 22, wherein the electrically attaching includes: 

 depositing the piezoelectric transducer layer as a piezoelectric ink onto the substrate to thereby cover the two spaced first terminals and the two second terminals; and curing the piezoelectric ink to form the piezoelectric transducer layer. The method of manufacturing a multi-function transducer assembly of claims 20 to 22, wherein the electrically attaching includes: depositing the piezoelectric transducer layer as a piezoelectric ink onto a mounting sheet; curing the piezoelectric ink to form the piezoelectric transducer layer; and bonding the piezoelectric transducer layer to the at least two spaced first terminals and the at least two second terminals. A method of controlling the multi-function transducer system as claimed in any of claims 12 to 17, the method comprising: controlling the speaker driving circuitry to supply a speaker signal to the spaced first terminals to thereby cause the piezoelectric transducer layer to operate as a speaker by emitting audio sound waves; detecting a terminating signal at a region of the piezoelectric transducer layer between at least two of the second terminals, wherein the termination signal is caused by a mechanical force applied to the region of the piezoelectric transducer layer; and controlling the speaker driving circuitry to terminate the supply of the speaker signal in response to detecting of the terminating signal. The method as claimed in claim 25, wherein the detecting includes: monitoring a voltage level across the at least two of the second terminals; and determining an occurrence of the terminating signal when the voltage level exceeds a voltage threshold value. The method as claimed in claim 25 or claim 26, wherein upon the termination of the supplying of the speaker signal, the method processes one or more input signals caused by distortions in the piezoelectric transducer layer. 28. The method as claimed in claim 27 wherein the processing includes creating an audio signal from at least one of the input signals. 29. A multi-function transducer assembly as hereinbefore described with reference to the drawings. 30. A method of manufacturing a multi-function transducer assembly as hereinbefore described with reference to the drawings.
